reason: SPEC_TRACKING.md faithfully tracks all 6 requirements (3 FRs + 3 NFRs) from the APPROVED SRS.md v2.0.0. Upstream B-2 caveats are correctly NOT propagated: (1) no 'Implementation Function (est.)' column or speculative content was carried over from SRS — the matrix is purely behavioral/acceptance tracking, and (2) no implementation function names are present, so the FR-02 taskq.executor.run vs taskq.executor.run_task naming inconsistency cannot surface. Canonical terminology is preserved verbatim: atomic write (tmp + os.replace), shlex.split, shell=False, TASKQ_TASK_TIMEOUT, TASKQ_RETRY_LIMIT, state machine pending→running→done/failed/timeout, exit codes 0/2/4/1, 6-char injection blacklist, p95<50ms, redaction regex (sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}|token=\S+) → [REDACTED]. Completeness check is internally coherent (3/3 FR, 3/3 NFR, 100% VERIFIED, 100% owned, 100% criteria-defined, 0 orphans). Out-of-Scope section faithfully mirrors SPEC.md §5: daemon, remote exec, non-JSON backends, circuit breaker, cache, concurrency. Lineage is explicit: PROJECT_BRIEF → SPEC.md v2.0.0 → SRS.md v2.0.0 (APPROVED round 1) → SPEC_TRACKING.md, with links to downstream artifacts (TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.md, TEST_INVENTORY.yaml).
citations: 01-requirements/SPEC_TRACKING.md:13-22 (Specification Status matrix — 6 rows, all VERIFIED, all owned by REQUIREMENTS_ENGINEER)
01-requirements/SPEC_TRACKING.md:34-39 (Out-of-Scope — daemon, 遠端執行, 非 JSON, 斷路器/快取/並發)
docs_embedded: Previous Sub-Task B-2 review JSON — SRS.md

gaps.message: Matrix adds a 'Decision Framework' column referencing 'harness v2.9 P3/P4' (e.g. 'harness v2.9 P3 (TDD)', 'harness v2.9 P4 整合測試') and notes like 'P3 須有獨立 benchmark 模組' / 'P3 須於 pyproject.toml 註冊 semgrep 自訂規則' that are not in the canonical SPEC.md. These are tracking-side planning hints rather than transcription errors, but downstream reviewers (TRACEABILITY_MATRIX) should treat them as advisory, not as SPEC-derived acceptance criteria. Recommend a one-line disclaimer noting the column is planning intent, not spec content.
